sql: SELECT products.* FROM products JOIN categories ON products.category_id = categories.category_id WHERE categories.category_name = 'Electronics';
SELECT SUM(total_amount) FROM orders WHERE order_date >= date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE);
SELECT product_name, rating FROM products ORDER BY rating DESC LIMIT 5;

SELECT customers.customer_id, SUM(orders.total_amount) AS total_spent FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id GROUP BY customers.customer_id ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 10;
SELECT product_name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC LIMIT 10;
SELECT product_name, rating FROM products ORDER BY rating DESC LIMIT 10;
SELECT employees.employee_name, COUNT(orders.order_id) FROM orders JOIN employees ON orders.employee_id = employees.employee_id GROUP BY employees.employee_name;

SELECT categories.category_name, SUM(order_items.quantity) FROM order_items JOIN products ON order_items.product_id = products.product_id JOIN categories ON products.category_id = categories.category_id GROUP BY categories.category_name;
SELECT customers.customer_id, SUM(orders.total_amount) FROM orders JOIN customers ON orders.customer_id = customers.customer_id GROUP BY customers.customer_id;
SELECT status, COUNT(order_id) FROM orders GROUP BY status;
SELECT suppliers.supplier_name, COUNT(products.product_id) FROM products JOIN suppliers ON products.supplier_id = suppliers.supplier_id GROUP BY suppliers.supplier_name;
SELECT orders.order_id, payments.payment_status FROM orders JOIN payments ON orders.order_id = payments.order_id;
SELECT DISTINCT customers.* FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.customer_id = orders.customer_id WHERE orders.order_date >= date_trunc('month', CURRENT_DATE) - interval '1 month';

nl: compare revenue this year vs last year
sql: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) AS year, SUM(total_amount) FROM orders GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date);
